pattern: Remembering the most recent index of each value turns the search for the tightest matching pair into a single pass that measures the gap between consecutive equal cards.
hints: The shortest window containing a duplicate must have that duplicate as its two ends.
For each value, only the distance between its two nearest occurrences can ever be the answer.
Walk the array keeping a map from value to its last seen index; whenever you revisit a value, the window length is the index difference plus one, and you keep the smallest such length.

edge_inputs.description: nearest pair is not the first repeat seen
single card cannot form a pair
adjacent duplicates give the minimum window
far duplicate then a closer one updates the answer

stress.description: large array over a moderate value range so duplicates appear at varied gaps
stress.note: value range scales with n to keep many duplicates
